title: quassia crocea
intro: Quassia crocea, also known as the yellow quassia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its bitter principles, quassin and amoorin, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Quassia crocea has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as to aid in digestion and relieve symptoms of anxiety and stress. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to harness its health benefits and medicinal properties.
